author: bharath.vedartham
content: moving this to <#692078615269212180> since its more relevant over there

author: potuz
content: <@908359967038382121> has found another issue that stems from the fact that we do not track payload equivocations. Late to fix this properly but I suspect that we should have added an  envelope_root to the signed envelope and just keep things referencing that HTR. In particular, instead of setting index=1, attesters would attest to that envelope root instead of the beacon block root as head. It would also resolve branches on equivocations. Something to keep in mind for the next iteration of forkchoice.

author: potuz
content: FWIW, the issue is that we should not `REJECT` attestations with `index=1` when the payload is invalid. There may be an equivocation with a valid payload. I'm pretty certain that there are a lot of edge cases when dealing with payload equivocations. Designing the pending payload queue in a non-dossable manner sucks because of this. cc <@363800010518822915> who wrote the PR.
